skill.name: codebase-search
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through efficient repository search using semantic questions, grep patterns, glob discovery, file reading, and Git history commands.
skill.summary: Find functions, dependencies, examples, and bug locations across large codebases.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell, network, credential, and heuristic patterns, but review shows most are Markdown examples in a documentation skill. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: the skill allows repository file access and Bash-based grep or Git commands, so users should apply normal shell quoting and repository-scope controls.

medium_findings.title: Bash Search Commands Require Input Care
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash and provides grep and Git command examples for repository search. These commands are expected for code navigation, but user-provided patterns and paths should be quoted before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and shell examples are direct evidence of external command use. The commands are ordinary developer commands, so the concern is operational misuse rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Repository File Access Is Core Functionality
medium_findings.description: The skill uses Read, Grep, and Glob to inspect code, configuration files, and documentation. This is necessary for the stated purpose, but repositories may contain secrets or sensitive proprietary code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and search workflow explicitly require reading and searching local repository files. This confirms a real data exposure surface, although it matches the advertised use case.

low_findings.title: Credential Pattern Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The API_KEY text is presented as an example search string, and process.env is shown as a pattern for finding environment variable references. No evidence found that the skill reads, stores, or transmits secret values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged strings appear inside instructional search examples. There is no surrounding code that accesses environment variables or credentials.

low_findings.title: Network Alerts Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are links to public documentation for ripgrep, regular expressions, and git blame. No evidence found of runtime network requests or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are Markdown reference links in the References section. They are not invoked by code and do not receive repository data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The line flagged for weak cryptography is the front matter description of the skill. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is plain metadata describing code search capabilities. It contains no cryptographic algorithm or implementation.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The analyzer combined shell examples, reference URLs, and credential-looking example text into a critical heuristic. Review found no code path that combines command execution, network transfer, and credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious elements exist only as documentation examples and reference links. No executable implementation or prompt injection attempt was found in the scanned files.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill demonstrates grep and Git commands that an assistant may run through Bash. The commands are standard search operations, but shell metacharacters in user input can create injection risk if not handled carefully.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: External command use is explicit and repeated in the documentation. The absence of executable wrapper code lowers confidence that there is an exploitable flaw in the skill itself.

content.user_title: Find Code Faster Across Large Repositories
content.value_statement: Large repositories make it hard to find the right files, call sites, and behavior quickly. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured search workflow using semantic questions, grep, glob, and file reading.
actual_capabilities: Chooses between semantic search, grep, glob, and file reading based on the question.
Finds function definitions, class definitions, imports, and usage examples.
Guides call site tracing and dependency exploration across related files.
Supports bug investigation by searching errors, handlers, tests, and data flow.
Uses Git history commands to inspect blame, file history, and code changes.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include a dedicated search engine implementation.
Search quality depends on available tools and repository indexing.
Shell examples require careful quoting for user-provided patterns and paths.
It cannot understand private code that the assistant is not allowed to read.

use_cases.title: Locate Feature Code
use_cases.description: Find where a feature starts, which files implement it, and which tests describe expected behavior.

use_cases.title: Trace Production Bugs
use_cases.description: Search error messages, handlers, call sites, and related tests to narrow the likely source of a defect.

use_cases.title: Plan Refactors
use_cases.description: Find callers, imports, tests, and dependent features before changing a shared function or module.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Function
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for a known symbol.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find where FUNCTION_NAME is defined and list the most important files that call it.

prompt_templates.title: Understand a Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Explore a feature without knowing exact file names.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search this repository to explain how FEATURE_NAME works. Start broad, then read the key implementation and test files.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate an Error
prompt_templates.scenario: Debug a specific message or failure mode.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find where ERROR_MESSAGE is produced, what inputs trigger it, and which tests cover the behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Map Refactor Impact
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced impact analysis before code changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace all callers, imports, tests, and data-flow dependencies for SYMBOL_NAME. Summarize the refactor impact and risky areas.

output_examples.input: Find where user registration is implemented.
output: Identifies the likely route, controller, service, model, and test files.
Summarizes the request flow from endpoint to persistence.
Highlights files to read next for validation and edge cases.

output_examples.input: Trace all usages of process_payment before changing it.
output: Lists direct callers and import locations grouped by area.
Notes related tests and integration paths.
Calls out high-risk consumers that need manual review.

output_examples.input: Find why an invalid credentials error appears.
output: Finds the error text and surrounding handler logic.
Suggests tests or logs that confirm the root cause.

best_practices: Start with a broad semantic question, then narrow with exact text searches.
Read surrounding context before drawing conclusions from a matching line.
Check tests and Git history when behavior or ownership is unclear.
anti_patterns: Running broad searches without file type or directory filters in very large repositories.
Treating a single grep match as proof without reading the related code.
Pasting untrusted search terms into shell commands without quoting or escaping.

faq.question: Does this skill modify code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on finding, reading, and understanding code before changes are made.

faq.question: Can it search unfamiliar repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. It starts with broad conceptual searches and narrows toward exact files and symbols.

faq.question: Does it require a special search index?
faq.answer: No. It can use standard assistant tools such as Read, Grep, Glob, and Bash search commands.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: It reads repository files and may run shell search commands, so sensitive files and untrusted patterns need care.

faq.question: Can it help with refactoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides caller searches, dependency checks, test discovery, and impact summaries.

faq.question: Does it send code to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed files contain only documentation links, not network request logic.
